steps.title: Interpret coefficients as side lengths
steps.content:
The equation $3x + 6y = 63$ models the sum of perimeters. For a regular polygon, perimeter $= (\text{number of sides}) \times (\text{side length})$.
$$\text{Perimeter of A} = x \times (\text{side length of A}) = 3x \Rightarrow \text{side length of A} = 3$$
$$\text{Perimeter of B} = y \times (\text{side length of B}) = 6y \Rightarrow \text{side length of B} = 6$$

steps.title: Match 6 to its geometric meaning
steps.content:
The coefficient 6 multiplies $y$ (number of sides of B), meaning it must be the side length of B. This matches the structure: $\text{number of sides} \times \text{side length}$ for perimeter.

A is correct.
